summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn continued the series on relationships, focusing on building a 'match made in heaven' marriage. He emphasized that such a relationship is built on earth through hard work, sacrifice, and biblical principles, not fairy-tale expectations. The sermon contrasted worldly, self-centered love with the biblical definition from 1 Corinthians 13, which is patient, kind, and not self-seeking. Pastor Travis outlined three key commitments for a godly marriage: first, a commitment to compromise and humility, modeled after Christ's self-emptying sacrifice. Second, a commitment to the marriage vow itself, rejecting a 'cancel culture' mentality in favor of a 'committed culture' that honors the promise 'till death do us part.' Third, a commitment to center the relationship on Christ, ensuring both partners are equally yoked in faith to build their lives on the solid rock of God's Word. He offered pastoral encouragement to those who have experienced divorce, affirming that God's grace defines them, and provided practical wisdom for singles seeking a godly spouse. The message concluded with a call to build all of life—whether single, married, or in business—on the foundation of Scripture and prayer.
mainPoints: A match made in heaven requires commitment to compromise and humility, following Christ's example of self-sacrifice.
A godly marriage is built on an unwavering commitment to the marriage vow, rejecting divorce as an option.
A lasting relationship must be centered on Christ, with both partners equally yoked in faith and building their lives on God's Word.

versesMentioned.text: Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking, it is not easily angered, it keeps no record of wrongs. Love does not delight in evil but rejoices with the truth. It always protects, always trusts, always hopes, always perseveres. Love never fails.
My dear brothers and sisters, take note of this: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.

Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility value others above yourselves, not looking to your own interests but each of you to the interests of the others. In your relationships with one another, have the same mindset as Christ Jesus: Who, being in very nature God, did not consider equality with God something to be used to his own advantage; rather, he made himself nothing by taking the very nature of a servant, being made in human likeness. And being found in appearance as a man, he humbled himself by becoming obedient to death— even death on a cross!
Follow God’s example, therefore, as dearly loved children and walk in the way of love, just as Christ loved us and gave himself up for us as a fragrant offering and sacrifice to God.

Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ. Wives, submit yourselves to your own husbands as you do to the Lord. For the husband is the head of the wife as Christ is the head of the church, his body, of which he is the Savior. Now as the church submits to Christ, so also wives should submit to their husbands in everything. Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her to make her holy, cleansing her by the washing with water through the word, and to present her to himself as a radiant church, without stain or wrinkle or any other blemish, but holy and blameless. In this same way, husbands ought to love their wives as their own bodies. He who loves his wife loves himself.
Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.

“Haven’t you read,” he replied, “that at the beginning the Creator ‘made them male and female,’ and said, ‘For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh’? So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
I tell you that anyone who divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another woman commits adultery.
Do not be yoked together with unbelievers. For what do righteousness and wickedness have in common? Or what fellowship can light have with darkness?

“Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock. The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had its foundation on the rock. But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand. The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell with a great crash.”
But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.

Monday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:4-5 - 'Love is patient, love is kind. It does not envy, it does not boast, it is not proud. It does not dishonor others, it is not self-seeking...'
Monday.action: Identify one way you acted self-seeking today. Intentionally choose an act of kindness for your spouse, family member, or a friend that puts their interests first.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to love others with Your patience and kindness today, putting their needs before my own.

Tuesday.scripture: James 1:19 - '...Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.'
Tuesday.action: In a conversation today, practice being 'quick to listen.' Focus fully on understanding the other person's perspective before formulating your response.
Tuesday.prayer: God, give me the grace to listen with empathy and respond with wisdom, controlling my tongue and my temper.

Wednesday.scripture: Philippians 2:3-4 - 'Do nothing out of selfish ambition or vain conceit. Rather, in humility value others above yourselves, not looking to your own interests but each of you to the interests of the others.'
Wednesday.action: In a situation where you disagree with someone, consciously choose to humble yourself. Look for a compromise or middle ground that honors the other person.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, You emptied Yourself for me. Help me to empty my pride and serve others with genuine humility today.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 5:21 - 'Submit to one another out of reverence for Christ.'
Thursday.action: Perform a practical act of service for your spouse or a close family member without being asked and without expecting anything in return.
Thursday.prayer: Father, teach me to submit and serve others as an act of worship to You, reflecting Christ's love in my relationships.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 6:14 - 'Do not be yoked together with unbelievers. For what do righteousness and wickedness have in common?'
Friday.action: Evaluate a key relationship or partnership in your life. Pray for wisdom about whether it is centered on and strengthened by a shared faith in Christ.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, guide me into relationships that draw me closer to You. Give me discernment to build my life on Your solid foundation.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 7:24-25 - '...everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.'
Saturday.action: Set aside time to read Scripture and pray with your spouse, family, or by yourself. Make a specific plan to apply one biblical truth you read to an area of your life or relationships.
Saturday.prayer: God, Your Word is my foundation. Help me to hear it and obey it, so that my life and my relationships will stand firm through every storm.
